name: aiohttp
what_it_does: Async Python HTTP client and server framework with native WebSocket, SSE, and streaming support built on asyncio.
use_cases: Sending high-concurrency async HTTP requests from an agent that manages many parallel tool calls
Consuming Server-Sent Events (SSE) streams from LLM APIs in real time
Establishing WebSocket connections to streaming data sources within an async agent
Tuning TCPConnector limits to control connection pool size under heavy agent load
Building a lightweight async HTTP server to expose agent endpoints without a full ASGI framework
not_for: Synchronous codebases where asyncio is not in use (use requests instead)
Production gRPC transport (use grpcio stubs instead)
Simple one-off scripts where the event loop setup overhead is not justified
best_when: Your agent is fully async and needs WebSocket support, SSE streaming, or very high connection concurrency from a single process.
avoid_when: You only need occasional synchronous HTTP calls — the asyncio event loop requirement adds unnecessary complexity.

auth.notes: Library — auth is implemented by the application via headers, BasicAuth helper, or custom connectors.

pricing.notes: Apache 2.0-licensed open source project under the aio-libs organisation.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: ClientConnectorError and ServerDisconnectedError are descriptive; aiohttp.ClientResponseError carries status, headers, and message for easy classification.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Stateless per-request; retry logic must be added by the caller (e.g., aiohttp-retry or manual exponential backoff).
known_agent_gotchas: ClientSession must be created once and shared across all requests in the agent — creating a new session per request leaks file descriptors and defeats connection pooling
Connector must be explicitly closed (await connector.close()) or used as an async context manager; unclosed connectors log ResourceWarning and can exhaust OS sockets
TCPConnector defaults to limit=100 total connections and limit_per_host=0 (unlimited per host); agents hammering a single host should set limit_per_host to avoid overwhelming the target
SSL verification is enabled by default but can be disabled via ssl=False on individual requests; this flag is easy to scatter throughout agent code accidentally
Response body must be consumed before the next request on the same connection; if an agent discards a response without reading it, the connection cannot be reused

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS verified by default; ssl=False disables verification per-request. Depends on yarl and multidict — keep dependencies updated. Secrets in URLs will appear in access logs.
